Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when one of its arguments is `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.min` function in JavaScript. It checks that when `Math.min` is called with the arguments `5`, `-7`, and `NaN`, the result is `NaN`. This test ensures that the `Math.min` function correctly returns `NaN` when any of its arguments is `NaN`, which is consistent with the ECMAScript specification. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `Math.min` function's behavior.
